verdict.answer_first: B: opens with the payoff, 'so your data stays correct even when things go wrong'; A opens with a definition, 'set of four guarantees'
verdict.decisiveness: B: lands one prescription, 'Reach for it whenever incorrect data would actually hurt'; A ends by offering multiple next paths, 'go deeper... isolation levels... ACID vs. BASE'
verdict.actionability: B: clearer trigger rule, 'when wrong data has real cost' and 'whenever incorrect data would actually hurt'; A is useful but more survey-like
